metadata.title: Build the Pipeline, Part 2: Clean, Save, Export
metadata.playlist: Shop for Ingredients — Collect Your Madison Data
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 3 brief — Shop for Ingredients: Collect Your Madison Data

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 4 of 7. You have three source nodes returning data. Raw data from three different sources is three different shapes, three different date formats, and a bunch of duplicates. The Cleanup node merges them, removes the duplicates, and standardizes the dates. The Save node writes the result to a file on your computer. Run the whole workflow once, save the file, then work from that file for everything else in the assignment — never run live again after you have your clean data. Collect once, reuse forever. Let's go.

props.segment: Build the Pipeline, Part 2: Clean, Save, Export
props.command: Merge 3 sources → dedupe → standardize dates → save file → export workflow JSON.
props.runningText: run once, save the file, never run live again…
output: Cleanup: merge + dedupe + YYYY-MM-DD + drop empty essentials
Save: CSV or JSON, one file on your machine
Export: workflow JSON for Canvas submission

beats.narration_text: The Cleanup step has four tasks: merge, deduplicate, standardize dates, and drop incomplete records. Merge: the three source nodes are producing separate output streams. Use a Merge node in n8n to combine them into one list. Deduplicate: decide what makes a record unique. For news items, the URL or the title is usually the deduplication key — two records with the same URL are the same article. Use n8n's Remove Duplicates node or a Function node to filter by that key. Standardize dates: all date fields must be in YYYY-MM-DD format. Different sources return dates as different strings — some say 'July 18, 2026,' some say '2026-07-18T10:00:00Z.' Use a Set or Function node to parse and reformat every date field to YYYY-MM-DD. Drop incomplete records: if a record is missing a title, a date, or a source name — the essential fields — remove it. A record with no date cannot be sorted. A record with no title cannot be identified. These are not optional fields.

artifactLines: 1. Merge: combine the 3 source output streams into one list (use n8n Merge node).
2. Deduplicate: remove records with the same URL or title — same key = same record.
3. Standardize dates: reformat all date fields to YYYY-MM-DD — use Set or Function node.
4. Drop incomplete: remove any record missing title, date, or source name.
Rule: a record with no date can't be sorted; a record with no title can't be identified.

beats.narration_text: The Save step writes your clean records to a file on your machine. n8n can output CSV or JSON. Both formats are acceptable for the assignment. If your A4 work will involve further data manipulation, CSV is easier to open and inspect in a spreadsheet. If it will involve code, JSON is easier to parse programmatically. Write to a folder you control — somewhere inside your project folder, not a temp directory. Name the file something like A3_data.csv or A3_data.json. Run the entire workflow — all six nodes — and look at the file. Open it in a text editor or a spreadsheet. Count the rows. Verify that the dates are in YYYY-MM-DD. Verify that there are no obvious duplicates. Verify that every record has a title, a date, and a source field. This visual inspection takes three minutes and catches most cleanup errors before the quality step in Episode 5.

artifactLines: Choose format: CSV (easier to inspect in spreadsheet) or JSON (easier to parse in code).
Write to your project folder — not a temp directory. Name: A3_data.csv or A3_data.json.
Run all 6 nodes. Open the file. Count the rows.
Visual check: dates in YYYY-MM-DD · no obvious duplicates · every record has title + date + source.
This is the file you submit and work from — protect it. Do not overwrite it after this run.
props.sparkLine: Run once. Open the file. Count the rows.

beats.narration_text: The last step in this episode is exporting the workflow to a JSON file for Canvas submission. In n8n, go to the workflow menu — the three-dot menu or File menu depending on your version — and select Export. n8n will download a JSON file containing the entire workflow configuration. Rename the file to LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json before you move it anywhere. Open the exported file in a text editor and search for your API key. If your key appears in the file as plaintext, you did not use n8n Credentials correctly — go back to Episode 3, add your key to Credentials, update the node to reference the credential, and re-export. Submitting a workflow JSON with a visible API key is a security error. The grader will see it. The instructor will see it. Fix it before submission.

artifactLines: n8n menu → Export → downloads workflow as JSON.
Rename: LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json
SECURITY CHECK: open the file in a text editor and search for your API key.
If the key appears as plaintext: go back to E3, move the key to n8n Credentials, re-export.
A visible API key in a submitted JSON is a security error — fix it before submission.
props.sparkLine: Export. Search for your key. Fix it if it appears.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Clean, Save, Export. Cleanup does four things in order: merge the three source streams, remove duplicates by URL or title, standardize all dates to YYYY-MM-DD, and drop any record missing a title, date, or source name. Save writes to CSV or JSON in your project folder. Run the entire workflow once. Open the file, count the rows — target is 50 to 200 clean records — and inspect dates and required fields. Export the workflow as LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json. Open the exported file and search for your API key before submitting anything. If the key is visible, it was pasted directly into a node, not stored in Credentials. Fix it. The two deliverables from this episode: the data file and the exported workflow JSON.

props.artifactTitle: Build the Pipeline, Part 2: Clean, Save, Export
artifactLines: Cleanup: merge → dedupe (by URL or title) → YYYY-MM-DD → drop empties.
Save: CSV or JSON, project folder, A3_data.csv / A3_data.json.
Run once: target 50–200 clean records. Open the file. Count rows. Inspect dates.
Export: LastName_FirstName_A3_Workflow.json — search file for API key before submitting.
Two deliverables from this episode: the data file + the exported workflow JSON.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. This prompt helps you write the cleanup rules before you build the Cleanup node. Paste it into Claude — fill in the bracketed fields with your actual data. The prompt reads: I have three data sources for an n8n workflow: Source 1 returns fields [list the fields from your RSS node output], Source 2 returns fields [list the fields from your API node output], Source 3 returns fields [list the fields from your CSV]. The A2 problem I am solving is [one sentence]. Write the cleanup rules for this data: the deduplication key, the required fields that must be present for a record to pass, the date field name in each source and the format it currently comes in, and the unified output schema I should target — field name plus format for each column. That output is what your Cleanup node needs to implement. Then take the cleanup rules back into n8n and build exactly what Claude described. Verify the output file matches the schema before you submit.

props.runningText: list the actual field names from each node's output before running this…
